threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train' like everyone else here. And good luck trying to find a seat during rush hour!
Caught a whiff of last Friday's jerk chicken from a takeaway on the 35. Smelled better than the bus, I swear. Brixton never sleeps, even on wheels.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I belonged was when my neighbor offered me homemade ajvar during a street barbecue. Nothing fancy, just pure, local generosity.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: The sound of live techno wafting from hidden backyard parties in Kreuzberg is like a siren's call—never knowing if it's a jam session or just a rehearsal.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their blank stare at BART delays—it's like background noise. Tourists panic; we just roll our eyes and keep scrolling.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy at the Civic Center Muni stop juggling while the bus was late again. It was the most San Francisco moment—chaos and creativity all in one.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about gaudí and tapas, but the real vibe is in the quiet plazas where locals sip vermut and chat for hours.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still stop for every pigeon on the street. Locals just weave through, not giving them a second glance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stare at the Tube map like it’s a treasure map, not realizing none of the lines go there anymore.
Caught a night bus last week, and a guy was trying to impress a girl with a packet of Skittles, claiming they were 'retro candy'. Awkward, but she seemed into it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting at the Tempelhofer Feld, I watched locals fly kites while an old man argued passionately with a pigeon. That's when I felt truly at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as a gallery, showcasing local artists while you munch on that juicy döner.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can navigate BART delays without breaking a sweat; the latest chaos is just another Tuesday for us.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus stuck behind a street performer who was juggling flaming torches. Only in SF do delays turn into a free show downtown.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that Gràcia's fountains are actually for filling up your bottle, not just for looks. They waste time buying water!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their bewilderment at the late-night tapa prices—still expecting cheap menus like in other cities.
